top_signals.title: Super Typhoon Bavi Puts China Ports, Commodity Flows and Pacific Vessel Supply at Risk
top_signals.summary: Super Typhoon Bavi is moving toward the Taiwan–China shipping corridor, raising the risk of short-term disruption to vessel routing, China port operations, commodity discharge schedules and Pacific vessel supply. According to ECMWF model output shown through Windy, Bavi is currently tracking as an extremely powerful typhoon system, with forecast wind gusts of up to 100 ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Taiwan’s CPI upside surprise shines spotlight on potential September rate hike
top_signals.summary: Taiwan’s inflation continues to pick up Taiwan’s June CPI inflation rose to 2.6% YoY, up from 2.2% YoY in May, coming in higher than forecasts (market: 2.3%, ING: 2.4%). This level was the highest inflation since January 2025, and marks a clear overshoot of the 2% inflation target. In terms of the sub-categories, unsurprisingly we ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Trump says interim accord to end war is ‘over’ after Iranian strikes
top_signals.summary: The U.S. president's remarks came after the two sides traded strikes again, marking the most serious threat to their interim peace deal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Weekly Dry Market Monitor: Week 27, 2026
top_signals.summary: Capesize C3 & C5 Supply vs Demand C3 (Tubarao–Qingdao): The current forward balance indicates cumulative vessel supply tracking below expected demand early to late July 2026, before gradually exceeding it into early August, indicating increasing forward vessel availability. C5 (West Australia–Qingdao): The current forward balance shows cumulative vessel supply tracking above expected demand across most ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Progress continues on WINDSEACON as subcommittee meets in person
top_signals.summary: Work on BIMCO’s WINDSEACON contract continues to move forward, with the subcommittee recently meeting in person to advance the drafting process and build on earlier milestones. The project aims to create a standard time charter party for the transport and installation of offshore wind turbines, supporting a sector that is expanding rapidly as demand for ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Mixed signals, research findings, and policy judgements − speech by Catherine L. Mann
top_signals.summary: Given at the Natixis CIB Private Debt Forum

top_signals.title: Clean tanker market climbs into overbought territory on flow optimism
top_signals.summary: Clean tanker freight is built on conversion. Dirty freight can reprice fast on ton-miles and risk premium, but clean has to move refinery output into exportable product and then into scheduled cargoes that fit laycans, discharge windows, and commercially acceptable tonnage. Q3 will come down to that conversion chain — where workable supply meets the ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: UK Carpet Maker Victoria Signs Deal With Left-Behind Bondholders
top_signals.summary: UK carpet maker Victoria Plc and a group of bondholders left behind in an earlier debt deal have struck an agreement over a balance sheet restructuring.

top_signals.title: Nigel Farage to quit UK Parliament, seek new mandate amid probes into crypto-linked gifts
top_signals.summary: Nigel Farage resigned as an MP amid two probes over gifts from Tether investor Christopher Harborne and convicted fraudster George Cottrell.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Strong Newbuilding Market Still The Case
top_signals.summary: More newbuilding orders kept pouring in during the past week, as owners maintained their investment “appetite”. In its latest weekly report, shipbroker Banchero Costa said that “newbuilding activity remained healthy this week, with dry bulk taking centre stage, while fresh orders were also reported in the container and gas carrier segments. In dry bulk, COSCO ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Fewer Accidents, Evolving Risks: INTERCARGO Highlights Progress and Priorities in Bulk Carrier Safety
top_signals.summary: Bulk carrier safety continues to show steady long-term improvement, but the nature of risk facing seafarers is becoming more complex and multi-layered. INTERCARGO’s Bulk Carrier Casualty Report 2026 covers the ten-year period from 2016 to 2025. Over that period, the global bulk carrier fleet grew from around 10,400 vessels in 2013 to 13,669 by December 2025. Against ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: The AI race is driving TMT debt issuance
top_signals.summary: We’re seeing a race to dominate the AI platform infrastructure, with operators of large technology platforms ramping up investments. We expect technology, media, and telecommunications (TMT) issuers to account for a large share of the €485bn total credit issuance expected for 2026. Given year-to-date issuance, we expect the TMT sector to raise an additional €55bn ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
